Title: Discovery of isoflavone phytoalexins in wheat reveals an alternative route to isoflavonoid biosynthesis

Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Chalcone-flavanone isomerase (CHI) family; contains conserved CHI motifs
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 expression system in N. benthamiana; CaMV 35S::AtMYB12 co-expression used to boost precursor supply
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate; Fusarium culmorum infection; biotic stress/PAMP datasets
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytoalexin-like defense metabolite with antifungal and antibacterial activity
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling; expression analysis
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports naringenin formation when co-expressed with TaCHS1, but is not essential for pathway output in N. benthamiana and is likely not an integral pathway gene in wheat
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Weak or no pathogen induction; differs from co-expressed cluster genes across development and biotic stress
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. chi-D1 is located in BGC4(5D) and can support naringenin production with TaCHS1 in heterologous assays. 2. Its expression is weakly or not induced by pathogen challenge or other biotic stresses unlike the core triticein genes. 3. The paper concludes chi-D1 is likely not an integral part of the triticein biosynthetic cluster.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it was experimentally tested in Results, but authors explicitly infer it is likely not an integral part of the triticein pathway; endogenous CHI activity or spontaneous chalcone isomerization can bypass it in N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Type III polyketide synthase; chalcone synthase
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream flavonoid entry step supplying naringenin branch
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: p-coumaroyl-CoA + malonyl-CoA (inferred for CHS); pathway output monitored as naringenin after isomerization
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Naringenin chalcone / naringenin precursor pool leading to naringenin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes chalcone formation at the entry to the flavonoid pathway, supplying precursor for naringenin and downstream triticein biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS metabolite profiling; pathogen-responsive expression analyses
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required upstream precursor-supplying step for triticein pathway; only pathogen-induced CHS among wheat CHS candidates analyzed
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Fusarium culmorum, MeJA and other biotic stress datasets
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Methyl jasmonate; Fusarium culmorum infection; other pathogen/PAMP treatments
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 in N. benthamiana; AtMYB12 co-expression increased pathway yield
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaCHS1 is the dedicated pathogen-induced CHS associated with BGC4(5D). 2. In transient assays it supplies the flavonoid precursor leading to naringenin. 3. Its induction pattern supports a defense-related role in triticein production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The isomerization step may be catalyzed by endogenous N. benthamiana CHI or occur spontaneously, so explicit CHI dependence was not demonstrated in planta.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms upstream intermediate for target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; mutant analysis of TILLING line Cad1684; LC-MS comparison with standards
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the first committed O-methylation toward triticein; loss causes naringenin accumulation and absence of downstream pathway metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Fusarium culmorum and broadly induced by biotic stress/PAMP datasets
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 in N. benthamiana; AtMYB12 co-expression used in some assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaOMT8 converts naringenin to isosakuranetin by 4′-O-methylation. 2. omt8 mutant wheat accumulates naringenin and lacks downstream triticein-pathway products. 3. TaOMT8 therefore initiates the methylation sequence required for triticein biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Only addition of TaOMT8 to the TaCHS1/chi-D1 combination substantially depleted naringenin and produced a prominent new peak, supporting a linear pathway.

Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for downstream CYP steps
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; mutant analysis of TILLING line Cad1682; LC-MS comparison with standards
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Catalyzes the second O-methylation toward triticein; loss causes isosakuranetin accumulation and abolishes downstream triticein-pathway metabolites
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Fusarium culmorum and other biotic stress/PAMP datasets
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: pEAQ-HT-DEST1 in N. benthamiana; AtMYB12 co-expression used in some assays
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaOMT3 methylates isosakuranetin at the 7-position to generate naringenin 4′,7-dimethyl ether. 2. omt3 mutant wheat accumulates isosakuranetin and lacks all downstream products. 3. TaOMT3 acts sequentially after TaOMT8 in a linear pathway toward triticein.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically distinct from TaOMT6/TaOMT7, which act later in the pathway.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Triticum aestivum; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates naringenin 4′,7-dimethyl ether at the 2′ position to form artocarpanone A
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor for isoflavone synthase step
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; purification and NMR identification of product; yeast microsome in vitro enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces the hydroxylated flavanone precursor required for TaCYP71F53-mediated isoflavone formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Fusarium culmorum and by multiple biotic stress/PAMP datasets
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaCYP71C164 is a flavanone 2′-hydroxylase in the triticein pathway. 2. It converts naringenin 4′,7-dimethyl ether to artocarpanone A in planta and in yeast microsome assays. 3. This hydroxylation creates the substrate required for the alternative wheat isoflavone synthase TaCYP71F53.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No apparent orthologs were identified in other grasses outside the Triticum/Aegilops D lineage.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Triticum aestivum; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes aryl migration coupled with C-ring desaturation to convert a 2′-hydroxylated flavanone into the isoflavone 2′-O-demethyl-triticein
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms immediate isoflavone precursor of target product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; purification and NMR identification of product; yeast microsome in vitro enzyme assay; analysis of two independent TILLING knockout lines Cad0227 and Cad1793
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Essential wheat-specific isoflavone synthase that enables an alternative route to isoflavonoid biosynthesis; loss abolishes triticein and causes artocarpanone A accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced by Fusarium culmorum, methyl jasmonate and multiple biotic stress/PAMP datasets
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key branch-defining enzyme / decisive isoflavone synthase step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaCYP71F53 is the specialized wheat isoflavone synthase responsible for the key branching step toward triticein. 2. It converts artocarpanone A to 2′-O-demethyl-triticein through a mechanism distinct from legume IFS enzymes. 3. Loss-of-function wheat mutants lack triticein and accumulate the predicted substrate, proving its essential role in planta.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for genome editing, cisgenics or intragenics to biofortify wheat with isoflavonoids; may also improve heterologous isoflavonoid bioproduction because legume IFS is often a bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catalytic activity depends on presence of a 2′-hydroxy group in the substrate; when upstream context is altered in N. benthamiana it produces a different flavanone side product rather than isoflavone.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Methylates the 2′-OH group of 2′-O-demethyl-triticein to form triticein
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Combinatorial transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS profiling of end product
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Completes triticein formation by terminal O-methylation of the immediate isoflavone precursor
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced by Fusarium culmorum and biotic stress datasets
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaOMT6 catalyzes the final 2′-O-methylation step from 2′-O-demethyl-triticein to triticein. 2. It functions as a terminal tailoring enzyme in the pathway. 3. It can also methylate alternative substrates to generate side products in heterologous expression assays.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Can promiscuously methylate other intermediates, contributing to side products such as arjunone and naringenin trimethyl ether in N. benthamiana.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Plant O-methyltransferase; tandem duplicate of TaOMT6 differing by one amino acid (G72R)
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes the same terminal 2′-O-methylation as TaOMT6 to produce triticein
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; LC-MS product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functionally equivalent backup terminal O-methyltransferase for triticein formation
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in public biotic stress datasets
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TaOMT7 is a tandem duplicate of TaOMT6. 2. It converts 2′-O-demethyl-triticein to triticein with similar efficiency. 3. It likely provides redundant terminal methylation capacity in the triticein cluster.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not included in the initial combinatorial experiments because of near identity to TaOMT6; later shown to have comparable activity.

Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Upstream phenylpropanoid/flavonoid precursor supply in heterologous expression system
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Key genes in phenylpropanoid pathway and upstream pathways (not specified in this paper)
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression in Nicotiana benthamiana with BGC genes; LC-MS quantification of triticein and naringenin
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased triticein yield 6.5-fold and more than doubled naringenin accumulation in heterologous assays
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Auxiliary enhancer of precursor supply in the transient expression platform, not a native wheat triticein regulator
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtMYB12 was co-expressed as a tool regulator in N. benthamiana to boost precursor supply. 2. It increased both naringenin and triticein accumulation in heterologous assays. 3. It is not presented as a native wheat regulator of triticein biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimentally used to improve product yield, but not a core subject gene for the wheat pathway itself; included here only because it directly affected nutrient-metabolite output in Results.
